title: An old photo you keep, and the story behind it
summary: Describe an old photo you keep, narrate the day it was taken, and explain why you have held onto it.
topic: An old photo you keep, and the story behind it
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) about an old photo you keep and the story behind it. Describe the photo, narrate the day it was taken, and explain why you keep it. Use past simple, past continuous, and present perfect.
hintStarters: I have kept the same photograph for so long that the edges have started to…
The day the photo was taken, my family was…
Although I have lost many things over the years, this one…

hintVocab.meaningVi: ảnh chụp tự nhiên, không dàn dựng
ở phía sau, trong nền ảnh
một vật đã cũ, đã qua nhiều năm sử dụng
giữ chặt, bám víu vào điều gì đó
tiếp tục bước tiếp, vượt qua

sampleText:
The photograph is unremarkable to anyone who does not know the story behind it. It shows three people standing outside a small railway station in rural Poland: my grandmother on the left, my grandfather in the middle, and my father — twelve years old and squinting into the sun — on the right. Nobody is looking at the camera. A dog sits just inside the frame, though no one in my family has ever been able to explain whose dog it was. The photo is creased down the middle from the years it spent folded inside my grandfather's wallet.

The day it was taken, my family was preparing to leave for the city. My grandfather had accepted a factory job in Wrocław, and they were saying goodbye to the village where both my grandparents had grown up. My grandmother was not crying in the photo, but my father told me she had been crying all morning. The train was running late. Someone — he could not remember who — had borrowed a camera, and one of the neighbours took the shot quickly, almost as an afterthought, before everyone went back to waiting on the platform. My father had never planned to keep the photo. He found it years later when he was sorting through his parents' things after my grandfather died.

I have had a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years. It is not a beautiful image, and nothing dramatic was happening when it was taken. But it has come to represent something I find difficult to put into words: the idea that the most important moments do not always look important while they are happening. My family has moved across three countries since that afternoon on the platform. They have built new lives and left others behind. Although I have never been to that railway station, I have looked at this photograph so many times that I feel I know exactly how the light fell that day, and what the air smelled like before the train arrived. Whenever I doubt that ordinary days matter, I look at the photo again and remember that nobody on the platform knew, at that moment, how much that quiet afternoon would mean to the people who came after them.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: unremarkable to anyone who does not know the story
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không có gì đặc biệt với bất kỳ ai không biết câu chuyện
nheo mắt nhìn vào ánh mặt trời
bị nhăn theo chiều dọc giữa
gấp lại trong ví của ông tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: preparing to leave for the city
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chuẩn bị rời đi đến thành phố
đã khóc suốt cả buổi sáng
gần như là một suy nghĩ chợt đến
lục lại đồ đạc của bố mẹ mình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the most important moments do not always look important
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những khoảnh khắc quan trọng nhất không phải lúc nào cũng trông có vẻ quan trọng
đã chuyển qua ba quốc gia

sampleAnnotations.phrase: built new lives and left others behind
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đã xây dựng cuộc sống mới và để lại những cuộc sống khác phía sau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: how the light fell that day
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ánh sáng đổ xuống như thế nào vào ngày hôm đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for background action at a specific moment
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'My family was preparing to leave for the city' dùng quá khứ tiếp diễn để mô tả điều đang xảy ra xung quanh một sự kiện cụ thể — hành động nền. 'Had been crying all morning' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành tiếp diễn để chỉ hành động kéo dài trước một thời điểm trong quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'My family was preparing to leave for the city' uses the past continuous to describe what was happening around a specific event — the background action. 'Had been crying all morning' uses the past perfect continuous for an action that had been going on before a past moment.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect for experience that connects past to present
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I have had a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years' và 'I have looked at this photograph so many times' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành để diễn đạt trải nghiệm hoặc hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn còn liên quan đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I have had a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years' and 'I have looked at this photograph so many times' use the present perfect to express experience or action that began in the past and is still relevant now.

sampleTranslationVi:
Tấm ảnh này trông không có gì đặc biệt với bất kỳ ai không biết câu chuyện đằng sau nó. Nó chụp ba người đứng trước một ga xe lửa nhỏ ở vùng nông thôn Ba Lan: bà tôi ở bên trái, ông tôi ở giữa, và bố tôi — mười hai tuổi và đang nheo mắt nhìn vào ánh mặt trời — ở bên phải. Không ai nhìn vào máy ảnh. Một con chó ngồi vừa trong khung hình, dù không ai trong gia đình tôi có thể giải thích được con chó đó là của ai. Tấm ảnh bị nhăn theo chiều dọc giữa vì những năm tháng nó được gấp lại trong ví của ông tôi.

Vào ngày tấm ảnh được chụp, gia đình tôi đang chuẩn bị rời đến thành phố. Ông tôi đã nhận một công việc ở nhà máy tại Wrocław, và họ đang chào tạm biệt ngôi làng nơi cả hai ông bà đã lớn lên. Bà tôi không khóc trong ảnh, nhưng bố tôi kể rằng bà đã khóc suốt cả buổi sáng. Tàu đến muộn. Ai đó — ông không nhớ là ai — đã mượn một chiếc máy ảnh, và một người hàng xóm chụp vội tấm ảnh, gần như là một suy nghĩ chợt đến, trước khi mọi người quay lại chờ đợi trên sân ga. Bố tôi chưa bao giờ có ý định giữ tấm ảnh. Ông tìm thấy nó nhiều năm sau khi đang lục lại đồ đạc của bố mẹ mình sau khi ông tôi mất.

Tôi đã có một bản sao của tấm ảnh này trên bàn làm việc gần mười lăm năm rồi. Đây không phải là một hình ảnh đẹp, và không có gì kịch tính xảy ra khi nó được chụp. Nhưng nó đã trở thành đại diện cho điều gì đó mà tôi thấy khó diễn đạt thành lời: ý tưởng rằng những khoảnh khắc quan trọng nhất không phải lúc nào cũng trông có vẻ quan trọng khi chúng đang xảy ra. Gia đình tôi đã chuyển qua ba quốc gia kể từ buổi chiều hôm đó trên sân ga. Họ đã xây dựng cuộc sống mới và để lại những cuộc sống khác phía sau. Mặc dù tôi chưa bao giờ đến ga xe lửa đó, tôi đã nhìn tấm ảnh này nhiều lần đến mức cảm thấy mình biết chính xác ánh sáng đổ xuống như thế nào vào ngày hôm đó, và không khí có mùi gì trước khi tàu đến.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect correctly with 'for'?
options: I have had a copy of this photograph on my desk since almost fifteen years.
I have had a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years.
I had a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years.
I have a copy of this photograph on my desk for almost fifteen years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect ('have had') + 'for' + duration expresses an action/state that started in the past and still continues. 'Since' is used with a point in time, not a duration.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'for' with a length of time; 'since' goes with a specific point in time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past continuous for a background action?
options: The day the photo was taken, my family prepared to leave for the city.
The day the photo was taken, my family has been preparing to leave for the city.
The day the photo was taken, my family was preparing to leave for the city.
The day the photo was taken, my family had prepare to leave for the city.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was preparing') describes an ongoing background situation at the time of a past event. Past simple ('prepared') would imply a completed action, not a background state.
mcQuestions.hint: Background actions happening 'around' a past moment use the past continuous.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the past perfect correctly for something that happened before another past event?
options: My grandmother was not crying in the photo, but my father told me she was crying all morning.
My grandmother was not crying in the photo, but my father told me she cried all morning.
My grandmother was not crying in the photo, but my father told me she has cried all morning.
My grandmother was not crying in the photo, but my father told me she had been crying all morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had been crying' (past perfect continuous) shows that the crying happened before the moment described by 'told me', which is itself in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Use the past perfect for an action that happened before another past action.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'although' to introduce a concession?
options: Although I have never been to that railway station, I have looked at this photograph so many times that I feel I know it.
Although I have never been to that railway station, but I have looked at this photograph so many times.
I have never been to that railway station, although, I have looked at this photograph so many times.
Although, I have never been to that railway station I have looked at this photograph so many times.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no comma directly after 'although', and no 'but' in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Although' introduces the subordinate clause; 'but' is not added in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option best describes a faded, well-worn photograph?
options: The photo is crease down the middle from the years it spent fold inside a wallet.
The photo is creased down the middle from the years it spent folded inside a wallet.
The photo has crease down the middle from the years it spent folded inside a wallet.
The photo was creasing down the middle from the years it spent to fold inside a wallet.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Creased' is the adjective form of the past participle here, describing the photo's current state. 'Spent folded' uses the past participle as an adjective after 'spent'.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'creased' as an adjective to describe the photo's current state.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses past simple correctly to narrate a specific completed event?
options: One of the neighbours was taking the shot quickly before everyone went back to waiting.
One of the neighbours has taken the shot quickly before everyone went back to waiting.
One of the neighbours took the shot quickly before everyone went back to waiting.
One of the neighbours had been taking the shot quickly before everyone went back to waiting.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple is used for a completed action at a specific point in the story. Past continuous or perfect are inappropriate for a quick, finished action.
mcQuestions.hint: For a single completed action in a narrative, use past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly expresses looking at something many times?
options: I looked at this photograph so many times that I feel I know it.
I have been look at this photograph so many times that I feel I know it.
I have looked at this photograph so much times that I feel I know it.
I have looked at this photograph so many times that I feel I know it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect ('have looked') + 'so many times' expresses repeated experience up to now. 'So much times' is wrong — use 'so many times' with countable actions.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'many' with countable nouns; 'much' is for uncountable nouns.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses a relative clause without repeating the subject?
options: A dog sits just inside the frame, though no one has ever explained whose dog it was.
A dog sits just inside the frame, though no one they have never explained whose dog it was.
A dog sits just inside the frame, though no one has ever explained whose dog was it.
A dog sits just inside the frame, though no one has never explained whose dog it was.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'no one', do not add 'they'. 'Whose dog it was' is correct indirect question word order. 'Has never' is correct; 'has never explained' needs no extra negative.
mcQuestions.hint: Indirect questions use statement word order: 'whose dog it was', not 'whose dog was it'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'find' in a past narrative context?
options: He has found it years later when he was sorting through his parents' things.
He found it years later when he was sorting through his parents' things.
He finded it years later when he was sorting through his parents' things.
He had found it years later when he was sorting through his parents' things.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple ('found') is used for completed action in a finished narrative. 'Finded' is wrong — 'find' is irregular: found/found.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Find' is irregular; its past simple form is 'found'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly describes something that is hard to express?
options: something I find difficult to put in words
something I find difficulty to put into words
something I find difficult to put into words
something I find difficult putting into words
mcQuestions.explanation: The fixed collocation is 'put something into words'. 'Find + adjective + infinitive' is the natural structure: 'find difficult to put'.
mcQuestions.hint: The fixed expression is 'put something into words' — always 'into', not 'in'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the present perfect to describe a series of life changes?
options: My family moved across three countries since that afternoon on the platform.
My family have been moving across three countries since that afternoon on the platform.
My family has move across three countries since that afternoon on the platform.
My family has moved across three countries since that afternoon on the platform.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect ('has moved') + 'since' + a past point in time. 'My family' is treated as a singular collective noun here, so 'has' is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Present perfect + 'since' for actions starting at a specific past point up to now.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a difference between keeping a photo because you love what it shows, and keeping it because you fear forgetting? Which do you think motivates most people who hold onto old photographs?
